blurb: A fat blue-green ball with crisp raised ribs edged in soft golden bristles and a woolly yellow crown. Clusters with age into a clump of blue balloons. (Genus best-guess for now.)

care.light: Bright light with plenty of direct sun keeps the blue colour and golden bristles strong; acclimate before a big jump in sun to avoid scorch.
care.water: Soak-and-dry: a deep drink in warm growth, then dry before the next. Keep it dry through winter.
care.temperature: Room temperature and warm in summer; keep above ~35°F and dry when cool.
care.dormancy: A summer grower; rest it cool and dry through winter to set up flowers.
care.feeding: A dilute low-nitrogen cactus feed once or twice in summer.
care.potting: Slow-growing; repot infrequently, handling with folded newspaper or thick gloves.
care.propagation: From seed, or by removing offsets once it starts to cluster.
care.bloom: Big satiny yellow flowers from the woolly crown on mature plants — the 'magnifica' payoff.
care.troubles: Rot from overwatering or cold-and-wet; sunburn if rushed into strong sun; mealybugs.
care.toxicity: Non-toxic — but the spines mean careful handling.
